title: Source register row
description: One row of data/sources.csv. The source register is the only route by which a source enters the repository. A footnote in a chapter, a source_id in a dataset row, and a citation key in references.bib all resolve to a row validated by this schema. See data-sources.md section 1.

source_id.description: Lowercase author-year-keyword identifier. Must equal the BibTeX citation key in references.bib and be unique across the register.

title.description: Title verbatim from the source, including any subtitle.

authors.description: Semicolon-separated 'Family, Given' entries in printed order, or a corporate author name. Expanded from the published record, never from memory.

organization.description: Institution or company responsible for the work.

publication.description: Venue, journal, conference, or document series. Use 'Web' for provider documentation.

publication_date.description: Absolute publication date. Where only a month is published, use the first day of the month and record the imprecision in notes.

source_type.description: Category of source. Determines the default evidence grade before the assignment rules in research-methodology.md section 4.2 are applied.

peer_reviewed.description: True only when the cited version is the peer-reviewed one. A preprint of a later-published paper is false until the citation is updated to the published version.

evidence_grade.description: A: independently verified. B: institutional primary research. C: provider-reported.

url.description: Direct link to the source. Must resolve at access_date.

doi.description: DOI where one is issued, preferred over url for citation. 'Not available' where none exists.

access_date.description: Date on which a contributor confirmed the URL resolved and the content matched this record.

notes.description: Preprint identifier of a published paper; page or section locator for a long source; conflicts with other sources; 'superseded_by:<source_id>'; date imprecision; verification status. Use 'None' when there is nothing to record, never an empty string.
